name: Room in Park Ave Historic District: Great area!
description: Superhost welcomes you to the "Pink Room": Peaceful, private room with comfortable, firm double bed in 1886 Victorian house in Park Ave Historic District. Room faces east, tall windows, central AC. Renovated kitchen, shaded back porch. One block from cafes, restaurants; near highways, East Ave District, Eastman Museum, RMSC, Zen Center, 1 mile from Memorial Art Gallery, 3 miles from U of Rochester. Driveway parking available depending on length of stay.
neighborhood_overview: Beautiful historic neighborhood with lots of trees, old houses, interesting architecture, neighbors walking dogs, close to Park Ave with lots of cafes, restaurants, small stores, 7-Eleven, CVS. Great for walking around and looking at architecture.
host_about:
I'm a semi-retired professor, in Rochester since 2003 I work at home, cook a lot and  work in the garden. I often go  to movies, music, and restaurants, or host my partner and friends. 

I hope you feel comfortable, while respecting my habits. I'm early to bed (by 10:30) and early to rise (by 7) person. I have a friendly basset hound named Rowan. I have traveled around the world and lived in Costa Rica, Chile, Germany, France, the UK.

name: Swiss Hospitality in the City
description: This is a guestroom with a queen size bed in a 3-bedroom house located on a quiet and tree lined neighborhood street. The bedroom is nicely furnished and is on the second floor. Large discounts for weekly and monthly stays!
neighborhood_overview:
You will find stores and restaurants within 3 Minutes walking distance. For breakfast you have at least 3 choices for which you can get details on the web: James Brown's Place, Morningstar (Greek Restaurant), and Donuts Delite. For a local lunch or dinner you may enjoy Johnny's Irish Pub, the Merchant's Grill, the Merchant's Pasta House, or Paola's Mexican Restaurant. Within a couple of miles you can find great food at a decent price at Sinbad's (Mediterranean Food including Vegetarian) in the Park Ave area or Sticky Lips (BBQ) on the corner of Culver/Atlantic. If you like a fancier place there is Lento, Two Vine, and Pomodoro that I can recommend.
My location is very central as the Downtown is about 3 miles to the West, the University of Rochester is about 7 miles away, Xerox Webster is 10 miles to the East, and Rochester General is 2 miles to the N. The George Eastman House is about 2 miles to the S. The public library with a playground is less than half a mile away. Lake Ontar
host_about:
I am a Swiss engineer who came to this country in 1984 to continue my studies. In 1991 I joined Xerox and have been working in R&D ever since. My main hobbies are skiing and boarding in the winter time and hang gliding and biking in the summer. I also love hiking in the Finger Lakes area and visiting vineries for a little tasting. The week of the Rochester International Jazz Festival is a great time to take off work, enjoy the wonderful music, and try to see as many bands as possible.
My favorite engineer joke: how do you know you met an extravert engineer? He looks at YOUR shoes!
I speak English and German fluently, enjoy practicing my French and Spanish, and with hands and feet may be able to communicate in Italian. Needless to say I enjoy hosting people from all corners of the world.
I retired in March of 2016, so now I am traveling a bit more exploring other Airbnb homes. What fun!

name: Budget-friendly for Laid Back Travelers
description:
Come spend some time in our community-minded housing collective. We offer low-frills accommodations for the traveler on a budget.
We're close by to Eastman School of Music, George Eastman House, and the Rochester Public Market.
neighborhood_overview: Close by to nightlife, parks, good eats, and cafes, the Eastman School of Music and George Eastman House, the Rochester Public Market, the Riverside Convention Center and much, much more!
host_about: Coming from all ages and walks of life, RocHostel spans eight spacious houses (all clustered together) and consists of 20 full-time residents, two cats, and a bunny rabbit- all passionate about sustainability, social justice, community-building, and making the world a better place in general.  We have a large and continually expanding garden, are active in our community, organize various events throughout the month, and operate RocHostel where we provide guests like you with budget-friendly accommodations and help them to make the most out of their visits to Rochester.  If you're looking for a laidback, low-key, social place to stay, you've found it!

name: Park Ave Historic District: Home Office Bedroom
description: new double/full size bed with firm mattress in sunny bedroom office in historic 1886 Victorian house with lovely art/antiques. One of 2 guest rooms, shared bathroom upstairs, 2nd shared bathroom downstairs. Friendly basset hound in residence. I often use this space daytimes depending on my schedule--but I work downstairs when guests are in. LGBTQI friendly. Near Park Ave cafes/restaurants, East Ave Historic District,   UR (3 mi.), RIT (5 mi.). One shared driveway parking space available.
neighborhood_overview:
LOCAL AMENITIES/DIRECTIONS
If you walk to the end of Donlon St, left out of the house, (it’s a dead end for cars) you can zigzag through the alley to Park Ave. To your right are: Baker St Bakery, Wine Sense wine store, Boulder Café, hair salons, dry cleaners, restaurants (Sinbad’s is excellent and cheap; Esan Thai across the street is also good as is the Mexican restaurants). To your left on Park Ave are CVS drugstore, a bank/ATM, and further down is a nice café, Jembetat, across the street from 7-11, a larger convenience store.

Heading north on Culver at University there is a no-frills grocery store Price Rite; heading east on Park Ave, if you follow it all the way to its end it curves around and dead ends onto East Ave. A couple blocks further east (to the right) you will find Wegman’s grocery store, a liquor store, Dunkin’ Donuts, a gym, etc.

Heading west on East Ave towards downtown, from Berkeley St you will come to Breathe Yoga next to the Eastman House

name: Luxuriously Remodeled 1BD Apt#2 in 1906 Mansion
description: Our most luxuriously remodeled & fully furnished 1 bedroom apartment on the 1st floor. Large, bright living room with 9′ ceilings, hardwoods, original moldings, & a fireplace, opens to a brand new kitchen, island with seating. Redesigned en suite marble bathroom & glass shower with entrance close to living room. Recently installed split/wall AC/Heat units in the living room and bedroom provide year-round comfort. Reserved off-street parking included. Walk to Park Ave stores/shops/restaurants.
neighborhood_overview:
This luxurious apartment is in a beautiful mansion on the corner of Argyle St & East Ave in the East Avenue Historic District. Within one block is Park Ave with numerous shops, cafes, restaurants, sights (George Eastman House, Memorial Art Gallery, Rochester Museum & Science Center) all within walking distance. Wonderful walking/biking neighborhood surrounded by mansions, an historic Frank Lloyd Wright restored home, Wegmans (Rochester's "Taj Mahal of grocery stores" according to Consumer Reports!), sightseeing, and many natural parks.

Located in one of Rochester's most scenic and vibrant neighborhoods, the East Avenue Historic District is near The George Eastman Museum (Kodak founder) surrounded by other mansions & historic homes with convenience shops, grocery stores, landmarks, cafes, restaurants, churches, parks within walking distance. If you like going out, you are directly near the heart of Rochester's nightlife with wonderful dining.
host_about: We’re both professionals, Ed's a retired teacher and Tom's a Realtor. As owners/occupants of a furnished 8-apartment historic property here in Rochester, we enjoy our guests/neighbors & also truly enjoy getting away to travel a few times/year. We appreciate quality, clean accommodations in safe, easy to walk/ride locations where we can explore interesting parks, great restaurants, and experience local culture.

The neighborhood is by  no means "upscale" or a "destination"- it's mixed socioeconomically, with some well-kept homes and other houses and businesses that could use some loving care. Guests have *not* had safety or security issues; we just recommend common-sense precautions like not leaving valuables in cars, locking exterior doors and latching ground floor windows (and using the optional alarm system, if you wish), and not walking alone at night.
host_about: I work in community development both as an employee of a community development credit union and as a property owner-manager committed to neighborhood revitalization that benefits everyone- especially long-term, lower-income residents.

neighborhood_overview: My home is in the 19th Ward, Sibley Tract. The 19th Ward Community Association is the oldest in the United States.  People from many backgrounds and walks of life live here.  Many of the doctors, professors and administrators employed by the University of Rochester live in my neighborhood, as well as several of our city counselors and county legislature. We are a diverse, age mixed, population.  U of R's College Town is close with restaurants, coffee shops, book stores, bars, yoga studio, hair salon and day spa.  Corn Hill, another historic area, is ten minutes away, and Susan B. Anthony's home and neighborhood less than five.

name: Fresh Retreat Off Of Park Avenue
description: We have the best of both worlds. We are on a quiet street right between the action of both Park and Monroe Avenue, only a short stroll to cafes, restaurants, and bars. Our spot in the Park Ave Neighborhood is a convenient 5 minutes into downtown Rochester and 10-20 minutes from the universities: St. John Fisher, Nazareth, U of R, and RIT.
neighborhood_overview: We are situated in the Park Ave Neighborhood within short walking distance to the Monroe Village Neighborhood. Park Ave is Rochester's best-known neighborhood for strolling and shopping in an urban environment. You’ll find an eclectic mix of residential and commercial properties, including restaurants, bars, retail, and salons.  Monroe Village is probably Rochester's funkiest neighborhood. Monroe Village after dark is a nightlife district with a casual, laid-back atmosphere. Lined with bars and independent restaurants, it is very much the place to go if you prefer jeans and t-shirt. By day, Monroe Village is a true indie shopping destination, with unique stores selling everything from antiques to trendy clothing to fair trade goods.
host_about:
Hi! My name is Katie and my husband Garrett and I host our home in the Historic Park Ave Neighborhood of the fantastic Western NY city of Rochester. I am originally from Miami FL and Garrett is from the small town of Mt. Morris in the greater Rochester region.

 Garrett and I met while attending Rochester Institute of Technology. Garrett went for mechanical engineering and I went for medical illustration. It's your typical nerd meets artist love story.

We frequently enjoy traveling to visit my family in South Florida. We are active with community service projects in our city. We also love movies, boardgames, biking, rock climbing, and snowboarding when the weather is right. Garrett attends rock climbing competitions in our area. You will find a few of my paintings on the walls of our home and maybe you'll also find a few on the walls of our city.

You can find fun things to see in Rochester on (Hidden by Airbnb) @explorerochester 

We are passionate about our neighborhood. We love our City of Rochester. We hope you get to enjoy everything this area has to offer.

neighborhood_overview:
You will find stores and restaurants within 3 Minutes walking distance. For breakfast you have at least 3 choices for which you can get details on the web: James Brown's Place, Morningstar (Greek Restaurant), and Donuts Delite. For a local lunch or dinner you may enjoy Johnny's Irish Pub, the Merchant's Grill, the Merchant's Wood Fired Pizza . Within a couple of miles you can find great food at a decent price at Sinbad's (Mediterranean Food including Vegetarian) in the Park Ave area or Sticky Lips (BBQ) on the corner of Culver/Atlantic. If you like a fancier place there is Lento, Two Vine, and Pomodoro that I can recommend.
My location is very central as the Downtown is about 3 miles to the West, the University of Rochester is about 7 miles away, Xerox Webster is 10 miles to the East, and Rochester General is 2 miles to the N. The George Eastman House is about 2 miles to the S. The public library with a playground is less than half a mile away. Lake Ontario with the Sea Breeze Am
host_about:
I am a Swiss engineer who came to this country in 1984 to continue my studies. In 1991 I joined Xerox and have been working in R&D ever since. My main hobbies are skiing and boarding in the winter time and hang gliding and biking in the summer. I also love hiking in the Finger Lakes area and visiting vineries for a little tasting. The week of the Rochester International Jazz Festival is a great time to take off work, enjoy the wonderful music, and try to see as many bands as possible.
My favorite engineer joke: how do you know you met an extravert engineer? He looks at YOUR shoes!
I speak English and German fluently, enjoy practicing my French and Spanish, and with hands and feet may be able to communicate in Italian. Needless to say I enjoy hosting people from all corners of the world.
I retired in March of 2016, so now I am traveling a bit more exploring other Airbnb homes. What fun!

host_about:

host_about:
A proud Torontonian, a lucky mother of three, a lover of travel, an avid reader, an adorer of beautiful things, a spiritual seeker, a creator of guilt-free, but still ridiculously delicious meals.
Always on the hunt for the best coffee, almond croissants, fish tacos and pizza.  Love to explore, love to adventure.  Love to connect with others.

As my husband Sam started spending more time south of the border for work, we quickly discovered that we really really loved the feel of this part of Rochester.  Every time I venture out on foot, I find something new, something beautiful, something unexpected.  There’s a little something here for everyone, and we are really excited for you to experience the same.

We’ve travelled to many places over the years (though it never seems quite enough!).  We’ve used Airbnb as our preferred choice for almost a decade.  Both in our own city, as well as clear across the globe, we’ve discovered that it’s such great way to travel.  We get to meet interesting and amazing people, live like locals, discover hidden gems in any given place, enjoy the comfort and flexibility of extra space and the comforts of home (like separate sleeping quarters for us and the kids, or a kitchen to prep quick meals and stash local treats we find along our way).  

So our goal is to host you the way we love to be hosted, bringing together the best experiences we’ve had and share them here with you in our very own way!

description: Stay in the heart of Park Ave, one of Rochester's best-known neighborhoods for strolling & shopping in an urban environment. neighborhood_overview:
Park Avenue is lined with specialty shops, small businesses of every description, and local restaurants and cafes that extend onto the sidewalks during the summer months. Neighborhood residents enjoy convenient access to daily necessities. Right on the Avenue are banks, salons, a pharmacy, bakeries, florist, bars, coffee shops, a veterinarian, a mailing/copy. Each August the Park Avenue Merchants Association hosts one of the oldest neighborhood festivals in the city, the Park Avenue Summer Art Festival, and each December rings in the holidays with the Park Avenue Holiday Open House.

All along Park Avenue and down its side streets are handsome homes of all shapes and sizes— single-family houses, beautiful brick apartment buildings, and converted multi-unit dwellings. From sprawling Tudor mansions to modest Princess Anne cottages, from the stately apartments to elaborate Queen Anne homesteads, nearly every block of the neighborhood holds historic treasures.

name: Quiet, Modernized Apt in Historic Arts District
description: Beautifully remodeled quiet apartment in the heart of Rochester's arts and cultural district.  Minutes walk from museums, performing arts theatres, restaurants, cafés, art galleries and micro-breweries.  Split bath allows for use of the vanity while toilet/shower is occupied.  All windows replaced in 2023.  Owners live on property. neighborhood_overview: Located in the Neighborhood of the Arts (NOTA), sites like the George Eastman Museum, the Rochester Museum and Science Center, the Strong (National Museum of Play), the Memorial Art Gallery, the Kodak Theatre, Rochester Auditorium Theatre, and many quaint music venues are a 2 to 20 minute walk away. Village Gate is a couple of blocks away and is a truly unique Rochester experience for food, drinks, and shopping.  There are seven local micro-breweries: Three-Heads, Rohrbach, Roc Brewing, Lost Borough, Sager Beer Works, Nine Maidens, and Fairport Brewery.  It's around one mile to the Rochester International Jazz Festival (June).  The entertainment district (East and Alexander), with many bars and clubs, is less than a mile away.

We look forward to many more years of exploring!

We love our city and we're happy to give recommendations for food, drink, and fun things to do.

name: Good location, Bright, South Wedge, AC and Parking
description:
We continue to clean fastidiously, and provide excellent ventilation.

Spacious 2nd floor flat with skylights, full kitchen and bath, living room, dining room and bedroom. There is Central Air conditioning and off-street parking. We are ideally located in the city of Rochester, close to Highland Park, the U of R and hospitals...and an easy walk to the cafes, (ice cream!) stores, pubs and restaurants of the South Wedge. Downtown in 10 minutes. Roc Cinema also reopened a short walk away.
neighborhood_overview: One great thing about this neighborhood is its easy accessibility to so many areas, colleges, hospitals and highlights of Rochester.  It is 10 minutes from the Eastman Kodak museum on East Avenue, 10 minutes from downtown, 10 minutes to Highland Park, 15 minutes to the University of Rochester (and the sprawling and beautiful Mount Hope Cemetery where both Susan B. Anthony and Frederick Douglass are buried...) 10 minutes to the suburb of Brighton, etc. Easy access to all the local festivals, whether you walk, bus, drive or Uber.
host_about:
I am a hatmaker (milliner), textile artist and grant writer, and I live in Rochester, NY.  My son Eli is a musician/videographer, and he sometimes lives here as well.  We like to go great places...we both have a lot of energy.  We both value creativity and respect that people have all kinds of various approaches to life. I believe that with consideration and communication we can figure it out so that everyone is happy.

I also teach felting classes, and indigo dyeing (shibori) classes - and hat making in my studio.  I like to have a bicycle to get around town for errands close by, and love walking my dog and getting to know neighbors and the neighborhood. 

I think it's important to get out and live life!  I also like to create safe and welcoming spaces to return to with textiles and art from around the world that are relaxing and beautiful.

host_about:
I am a long time Rochester Resident. My love of travelling has taken me all over the world, but I always love coming back home to Rochester and its beautiful 4 seasons weather.

I am a professional photographer and you would enjoy some of my photographs in my properties.
I have seen my fair share of hotels and Airbnb houses on my travels around the world and understand the needs of fellow travellers. As a host, I can’t wait to show fellow travellers the same great hospitality that I have had across the globe.

My college town properties are located at a walking distance to the U of R and Strong memorial hospital, highland hospital and highland park. These are specifically organized to cater to professionals like physicians, nurses, medical students and other U of R visitors so they can live in a convenient place to work and can walk to their work or interviews etc. these are also well suited for guests visiting family locally or at these hospitals or coming to town for one of many festivals including world famous Lilacs festival. name: Spacious 2 Bedroom in Vintage 1910 Southeast Home
description: Enjoy a spacious, private apartment in an old home nestled in a quiet corner of Southeast Rochester with culture and nightlife nearby. Located in Swillburg, it is near the city center, U of R, and Rochester’s many museums, universities, and restaurants. Inside, relish in the memory foam beds, renovated full kitchen, claw foot tub, comfortable furnishings, and blazing fast Wi-Fi. And, with the owner living on site, it’s like having a friendly neighbor upstairs. You’ll always feel safe.
neighborhood_overview:
Swillburg is a great little family neighborhood nestled between two major streets and full of great shops, restaurants, coffee houses, and bars. The charming, narrow streets and natural mixture of homes and businesses offer the feel of a village with the convenience of city living.

Swillburg is conveniently between the South Wedge and Park Ave neighborhoods, and very close to the East End and Neighborhood of the Arts, where most the life is, yet far enough that you don’t have to listen to it while you try to sleep! Swillburg is one of the safest areas in the city and the home is located in the quiet residential only section.

A typical evening in Swillburg might include a leisurely walk through a community garden, a bite to eat at a local establishment, or a movie at the beautifully-restored Art Deco Cinema Theater.
host_about: I’m a professional organizer and own An Eye For Detail in addition to being an Airbnb host. neighborhood_overview:
Historic Corn Hill, or as it was dubbed, "the ruffled shirt district" when established in the mid 1800's, is located on the west bank of the Genesee River and adjacent to downtown Rochester. It is a residential neighborhood with restaurants and shops along the river, a 5 minute walk. Corn Hill is unique because of a strong neighborhood association and the famed Corn Hill Festival, occurring in mid July and the Holiday Historic House Tour, held in early December.
In general, Rochester has non stop festivals from mid May (Lilac Festival), the 10 day International Jazz Festival in June, on through September, (Fringe Festival), and right up to the holidays.
host_about:
As a native Rochestarian, and 25 years in this home, I know of many of the local gems and can help you get around Rochester.  Whether you're in the pursuit of food, culture or nature walks, I welcome the opportunity to help make your stay satisfying.  I can be as visible or invisible as you'd like. I enjoy meeting people of different backgrounds, exchanging stories and ideas.  I've traveled to several locations in Europe, New Zealand, Tasmania and Central America, along with fairly extensive travel throught the U.S.  
My education and training from Syracuse University was in Clinical Social Work. My career was at the University of Rochester Medical Center. Now I'm retired and spending time volunteering and exploring. 
My life motto is "We all do better, when we all do better."

Many attractions and activities near the unit should be taken advantage of while staying with us. The Rochester Philharmonic Orchestra, the National Museum of Play, and the George Eastman House International Museum of Photography and Film are just a few of the cultural institutions that populate the area. Enjoy the royal charm and vivacity of this Queen Anne--style mansion built in 1907---over 113 years ago. When George Eastman founded Eastman Kodak on September 4, 1888, he later had many homes built near Kodak Park to attract executives during the early growth period. The Royal Escape is one of those homes. Whether you plan to work or escape during your stay, take the time to experience the same historic neighborhood that inspired such international leaders as Susan B. Anthony and Fredrick Douglas. This home is for those who appreciate the historic luxury of centuries past.
Inside you will find a Mansion and Tour Guide for historical insights and places to visit to enhance your enjoyment during your stay. In addition, the Guest Rules are included to help you locate the specific information you may need about your accommodations.

I've lived in this neighborhood for 19 years. This area has a very mixed socioeconomic demographic, with long term residents, immigrants, and college students living side by side. This area has its challenges like anywhere else, but overall it's a great neighborhood. If you want complete silence, or if you get uncomfortable when you see people who look different than you, this is not the house for you. Please book somewhere else instead.

It is generally fairly quiet except for the background hum of traffic, the occasional thumping bass, firetruck sirens in the distance, a few dogs, and in the summer, some fireworks here and there. description:
Based in the SouthWedge minutes from downtown Rochester. This house has been well-loved. I chose to share this space and remain in my apartment around the corner. This is a great neighborhood with a menagerie of sounds, sights, and surprises. I hope you enjoy it as much as I do. neighborhood_overview:
South Wedge is one of the city’s oldest neighborhoods. South Wedge is home to two historic districts and was once home to several historical figures including Frederick Douglass and jazz legend Cab Calloway. Today, South Wedge is one of Rochester’s trendiest enclaves. South Wedge merges the old and new with condos in renovated school buildings, brownstone apartments, modern townhomes, and colorful Edwardian and Victorian houses. The unique mixture of rental options is complemented by South Wedge’s vibrant murals and other forms of art (the area has the most public art in the city outside of the Neighborhood of the Arts).

South Wedge is also known for its strong sense of community. South Wedge has two neighborhood newspapers and several neighborhood organizations that hold popular events every year like Third Thursday Concerts and Night of the Living Wedge, a costume contest, and a pub crawl. The majority of South Wedge’s businesses are locally owned, adding to the close-knit

neighborhood_overview:
Charlotte is a quaint little community right on the edge of Lake Ontario! Home to the Ontario State Park (just a few blocks from your space) for swimming, picnic, and playground!

Also a few blocks away are many local favorite restaurants, both high end and "sports bar" environments, enjoy Abbotts Custard, and several attractions including a tour of the 1822 Charlotte Genesee Lighthouse!
If you're looking for things to do either while here or if you arrive early, check out my guestbook, I highly recommend Mr. Dominicks at the lake for great food and cocktails. Hose 22 or Windjammers are great local sports bars with fantastic food, beer and wine. Tour the Genesee lighthouse, stroll down the pier, enjoy Ontario State Park and ride on the genuine historic carousel!

neighborhood_overview:
Charlotte (pronounced shar-lot) is an amazing place filled with great restaurants, activities, desserts and most of all the LAKE! You're a short drive to the city to experience all the great things Rochester has to offer. Or head down the lake to hiking and bird watching spots that will have views for days.

Charlotte neighborhood enjoy the best of all possible worlds -- a scenic resort village situated inside a world-class city. A very active and cohesive Neighborhood Association completes the small-town feel. In summer, locals and visitors flock to Ontario Park Beach, music concerts and special events like the Rochester Harbor and Carousel Festival. Each winter, the Lakeside Winter celebration draws hundreds to the "Chilly, Chili Challenge" and "Polar Plunge."

The Strong neighborhood in Rochester, also known as the "White Coat area," is a vibrant community located near the renowned Strong Memorial Hospital and the University of Rochester Medical Center. This neighborhood exudes a sense of vitality and intellectual energy, as it is home to a diverse population of healthcare professionals, researchers, and students.

The Strong neighborhood offers a unique blend of residential and commercial spaces. Its tree-lined streets are lined with charming houses, ranging from historic properties to modern apartments, catering to different lifestyles and preferences. The area's proximity to the medical center makes it a sought-after location for those working or studying in the healthcare field.

name: Renovated 4BR | Historic Charm | Prime Location |
description:
Welcome to your newly renovated 4-bedroom home in the heart of Rochester's eclectic South Wedge neighborhood! Built in 1860, this historic yet modern home offers an open-concept layout and a range of updated amenities to make your family or group stay both comfortable and memorable. Perfectly located, it’s just minutes from key Rochester landmarks.

We apologize for the inconvenience but are not accepting guests without prior favorable ratings on Airbnb or large groups on business trips.
neighborhood_overview: The South Wedge is eclectic and creatively evolving- one of Rochester’s longstanding neighborhoods, home to two preservation districts, and some of the oldest houses in the city as well as renovated historic multi-family housing such as the Ellwanger-Barry School, which is now condominiums. From the South Wedge you can easily access Center City, Monroe Avenue, the University of Rochester and Medical Center, Mt. Hope Cemetery, and the Genesee River. Just around the corner are Highland Park and Genesee Valley Park. South Avenue carries a distinctive Brooklyn vibe with thriving businesses. You can attend a concert at the historic German House; have a drink or meal at one of the many bars, restaurants, or delis along South Avenue; schmooze with neighbors at a local coffee house; shop at various boutiques; drop in on a yoga class; at the South Wedge Farmers Market you can buy local and organic products.
host_about: Happy couple that enjoy both staying at home with our mixed family of kids and pets and also love traveling and often leaving them behind.

name: 1886 City Cottage in South Wedge Neighborhood
description: This spacious 1400 sq. ft. cottage was built in 1886 and has been fully renovated, maintaining the historic charm but adding contemporary amenities. Centrally located in the South Wedge neighborhood, steps away from an organic grocery store and shops, restaurants and bars. Close to downtown Rochester, Highland Hospital, Strong Memorial Hospital, University of Rochester, and the city's cultural district. Directly across the street from one of our other airbnbs for large families!
neighborhood_overview: The South Wedge Neighborhood is conveniently located close to downtown Rochester and is minutes from Strong Memorial Hospital, Highland Hospital, and Golisano Children’s Hospital. The neighborhood includes a natural foods grocery store across the street from the house, many restaurants and shops, a craft brewery, and many nightlife options. Highland Park and Mt. Hope Cemetery are within walking distance and offer beautiful scenic views.
host_about: I’m a small business owner who lives in the South Wedge neighborhood near our two Airbnb properties. My interests are history and art, I love to do historical research and also enjoy anything creative. My partner, Jim, and I have enjoyed restoring our home and the Airbnb’s and have tried to maintain some of their original features and respect the era in which they were built. Our business, Historic Houseparts, is a retail business that specializes in architectural salvage, restoration supplies and vintage decorative accessories. We’ve used lots of our products in our houses. We also love to travel! So we’ve tried to make our houses feel like places we’d love to stay if we were traveling.

Please reference my guidebook for a list of local restaurants, bars, entertainment, parks, museums, sightseeing attractions, and other unique activities.

name: Private Bedroom and Bath with Separate Entrance
description:
This calm bed and bath in the back of our house on the SECOND FLOOR is a great place to stay while you explore Rochester. You enter through the back porch and MUST GO UP A FLIGHT OF STAIRS to the suite.

Located on the edge of the South Wedge/Highland Park area of Rochester it is walking distance to cafes, restaurants, Highland Park and the thruway 490.
It is easy to get to the local schools RIT, U of R, and the hospitals - as well as to downtown! Bus lines are close if you don't have a car.
neighborhood_overview:
You can walk up Mount Vernon at the corner for 10-15 minutes right into Highland Park. Designed by Frederick Olmsted, it is amazing.
If you head the other direction, toward downtown on South Avenue you will find a cafe, a new sushi bar, a co-op grocery store, and my favorite ice cream and chocolatier “the Hedonist.” There is often a line- but it moves well - and their homemade flavors and cones are great.
If you turn left toward Meigs from our house you can walk uphill to a back entrance to Highland Park, or turn left and find Dicky’s corner pub. I love their Friday fish fry! You can have it breaded, fried or grilled…
Farther down Meigs at South Clinton is a new restaurant called “Crisp,” and it gets great reviews...and it IS open on Sunday and Monday nights. Kitty corner from "Crisp" at the big old brick church is the Swillburger - with good burgers etc., a bar, and games! Ie. Pinball etc…
We also love Pizza Wizard, down South Clinton on the right one block (next
host_about:
I am a hatmaker (milliner), textile artist and grant writer, and I live in Rochester, NY.  My son Eli is a musician/videographer, and he sometimes lives here as well.  We like to go great places...we both have a lot of energy.  We both value creativity and respect that people have all kinds of various approaches to life. I believe that with consideration and communication we can figure it out so that everyone is happy.

I also teach felting classes, and indigo dyeing (shibori) classes - and hat making in my studio.  I like to have a bicycle to get around town for errands close by, and love walking my dog and getting to know neighbors and the neighborhood. 

I think it's important to get out and live life!  I also like to create safe and welcoming spaces to return to with textiles and art from around the world that are relaxing and beautiful.

name: Boho, Mid-Century 1870 Farmhouse
description: Welcome to your own tranquil oasis, an exclusive whole unit designed for your ultimate living experience. Located minutes from Strong Hospital, Highland Hospital, and the University of Rochester. Explore the nearby Highland Park and South Wedge for dining and shopping. In-unit laundry and premium stainless steel appliances. Immerse yourself in the captivating fusion of Boho and mid-century modern styles within our historic 1870 farmhouse. Embrace modern comforts with timeless charm.
neighborhood_overview:
This property is located in the South Wedge on Linden Street- a federally designated historic street. You'll find plenty to do within a short walk from the property (enjoy looking at all the funky painted houses and interesting architecture you see). Down the street on South Ave you'll find restaurants, bars and shopping. Going in the other direction on South Ave you'll see Highland Park (smell the famous lilacs if they are in bloom!) and Highland Hospital.

On Mount Hope Avenue (the other cross street) you'll see the Rochester Collegetown, which has plenty of shopping, bars and restaurants. If you continue down Mount Hope and fork left onto 15-A you'll find a Costco. If you continue in this direction you'll end up at Marketplace mall. Mount Hope also has a brewery nearby call Switfwater brewery.

Just a few minutes drive away you can find the Strong Museum of Play, the Rochester downtown, and one of the famous Wegmans Food Markets Stores.

neighborhood_overview: The Upper Monroe neighborhood has all the ingredients essential to comfortable city living - retail, restaurants and recreation - within walking distance of the house. Its boundaries are marked at the north by Route 490, south at the city line with the Town of Brighton, east on Culver Road, and west to Field Street.  The Monroe Avenue Strip, inside the Monroe Upper Village, includes a pet store, shoe repair, family market, hair salon, a gaming store and much more. Many Upper Monroe neighbors enjoy the weekly walk to the Monroe Village Farmers' Market, held each Wednesday at the Blessed Sacrament Church parking lot at Monroe Avenue and Oxford Street. Cobbs Hill Reservoir is a quick walk, as well as hiking on beautiful Pinnacle Hill just a few blocks from your front door. Bordering neighborhoods, such as Highland, Swillburg and Park Ave, are also some of the nicest in Rochester.

host_about:
Although I take life seriously I find a lot of humor in myself and the world around me. I love good food, natural fun and intellectually stimulating conversation. I DON’T like the gym! But,  I LOVE cake so I’m trying to find the right balance with that. Luckily  I have pretty good genes. I live life by designs so I always feel like I’m working on something. Whether it’s building my businesses, friendships or creating new memories, my journey so far has been quite colorful and enlightening.

I love entertaining friends at home, celebrating family/friends milestones, dining out, going to new museums, live music AND sporting events. I also like meeting new people, traveling, and laughing at silly commercials and off-beat jokes.

I can sometimes travel a great deal for work. I have been to Singapore, Paris, Brussels, and throughout the Caribbean (Jamaica, Aruba, Antigua, Bahamas...etc) and most recently Europe (Germany, Italy,  Czech Republic).

neighborhood_overview: The South Wedge is said to be the fastest-growing neighborhood in the state outside New York City and is highly regarded for its strong sense of community. It boasts several neighborhood organizations and two newspapers (The Wedge and The South Wedge Quarterly). The majority of homeowners take pride in the neighborhood, and it has an ever increasing number of interesting shops and bars. Several streets are lined with multicolored "painted ladies" homes reminiscent of San Francisco. The South Wedge is also noted for having the most public art in the city outside the Neighborhood of the Arts. South Avenue and surrounding streets have a bright variety of art totems created by area artists, transforming parking meters into sculptures. In 2013 the Wall\Therapy project added nine murals to blank walls throughout the Wedge.

neighborhood_overview:
The beautiful Corn Hill Neighborhood is considered the “Oldest Residential Neighborhood in Rochester, NY”, and is also one of its most unique. We are located on the banks of the Genesee River and a short walk from our city’s business district.

Once known as the Third Ward, Corn Hill is a showplace of historic architecture, manicured streets and inviting parks. As a congenial and diverse community, we are committed to protect and promote the character of our area by nurturing goodwill and friendship among our neighbors.

Corn Hill is well known today for its annual events. Each August for two days, more than a quarter million visitors pack its narrow streets for the Corn Hill Art Festival which originated in 1968. Also, each summer, the Clarissa Street Reunion brings current and former residents together with visitors for a music-filled event on a street that was once referred to as “Rochester’s Broadway” because of its jazz clubs. neighborhood_overview:
The White Coat area in Rochester likely refers to the Medical Center District, particularly around the Strong Memorial Hospital and the University of Rochester Medical Center. This region is often called the "White Coat District" due to the large number of medical professionals who work and study in the area, wearing their white lab coats.

It's a hub for medical research, healthcare, and education. You'll find top-notch hospitals, cutting-edge research facilities, and medical schools, making it a prominent center for healthcare in the region. The area typically has a mix of modern medical buildings, research facilities, and green spaces, creating a professional and academic atmosphere. CVS, Barnes and Noble are walking distance from the home. If you don’t feel like making breakfast, you can walk a block to the Mount Hope Diner. There are other nice restaurants within walking distance to the home!

It is a revitalized landmark community full of restored 19th century homes in a variety of modest and vintage styles that are blended well with 20th century townhouses and condominiums. In most cities these kinds of neighborhoods are only accessible by the wealthy but here in Rochester, people from all walks of life make Corn Hill their home

Regardless of their backgrounds, people are drawn to Corn Hill for its history, proximity (walking distance to downtown and the University of Rochester) and for its sense of community

The Corn Hill Neighbors Association is one of Rochester’s most active neighborhood associations, working hand in hand with city government and other neighborhood groups to provide for the continuing improvement of the neighborhood. neighborhood_overview:
A sprawling neighborhood immediately east of Downtown Rochester, Alexander is named for the main thoroughfare in the area lined with restaurants, bars, museums, and apartments that range from affordable to upscale. Historic East Avenue is an upscale community with several stately mansions and diverse architectural styles such as Victorian, Art Deco, Italianate, and Gothic Revival. From the Rochester Museum & Science Centre to the George Eastman Museum to Memorial Art Gallery, there’s always something to explore in this historic part of Rochester. Locals enjoy strolling along East Avenue and surrounding streets to the many British and Irish-inspired pubs that give this neighborhood a sense of nightlife just minutes from the city center.

Minutes to all the major hospitals, such as Union, Strong Memorial and Rochester General.

12 minute drive to Rochester Airport.
The neighborhood is easy to get around and there is much more for you to enjoy your stay as much

neighborhood_overview:
The 19th Ward known as "Urban by Choice" is the neighborhood slogan for this southwest corner of the city. With 22,000 residents, the 19th Ward is the largest residential neighborhood in the city and has the oldest continuously operating neighborhood association in Rochester.
The 19th Ward holds many social events including a Square Fair in June, annual convention in November, and lively community potluck dinners. The Westside Farmer's Market is a popular stop at St. Monica's church. This neighborhood has its own business association and a number of block clubs.
Proximity to facilities such as Genesee Valley Park, the University of Rochester, the Erie Canal, Brooks Landing riverfront development, the Greater Rochester International Airport, and Interstate 390 makes the 19th Ward a convenient and attractive place to live.

neighborhood_overview:
The University of Rochester is located in the Neighborhood of the Arts, a vibrant and historic district known for its theaters, museums, and art galleries. The neighborhood is also home to a variety of shops, restaurants, and cafes.

The Neighborhood of the Arts is a great place to live if you're looking for a walkable and bikeable community with a lot to offer. It's also close to the university's River Campus, making it a convenient place to live for students and staff.

Here are some of the things you can do in the Neighborhood of the Arts:

Visit the Memorial Art Gallery, one of the oldest and largest art museums in New York State.
See a show at the Geva Theatre, Rochester's oldest professional theater company.

neighborhood_overview:
Discover the Charm of Corn Hill

Nestled along the scenic Genesee River, Corn Hill is one of Rochester’s most historic and picturesque neighborhoods — blending classic 19th-century architecture with modern townhomes and riverside apartments. This vibrant community offers the perfect balance of heritage and contemporary living, just minutes from downtown and the University of Rochester.

Corn Hill’s warm, welcoming atmosphere is one of its greatest strengths. The Corn Hill Neighbors Association plays an active role in keeping the area safe, beautiful, and community-focused. Along the riverfront, Corn Hill Landing features some of Rochester’s favorite dining spots and elegant river-view residences.

Throughout the year, Corn Hill hosts several beloved local events:

The Corn Hill Arts Festival, a two-day celebration each August that draws thousands of visitors to enjoy art, music, and food.
